title: pedicularis diffusa subsp. diffusa
intro: Pedicularis diffusa subsp. diffusa, also known as common lousewort, is a perennial flowering plant species within the broomrape family, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Pedicularis diffusa subsp. diffusa include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as acting as a natural remedy for digestive issues and respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in infusions and decoctions for its medicinal properties.
